Manually trigger a daily digest for a specific trip day.

Useful for debugging, re-running after a prompt change, or backfilling

set a different day_number or pass fresh data to force a re-run.

Normally fires automatically when trip status → completed, but this

Manually trigger cross-day planning-context synthesis for a trip.

Normally auto-fires at the end of each daily digest; this endpoint lets

current output without waiting for the next daily run.

schema.title: Response Get Trip Planning Context Admin Trips  Trip Id  Digest Planning Context Get

get.description:
Report ingestion provider configuration and per-source experience counts.

Returns which providers are configured (have valid credentials),

List published angles for a place, ordered by priority + distinctiveness.

Passing ``user_id`` overlays per-user personalization: each angle
picks up a ``why_for_you`` hook and ``personalization_rank`` from
the reader's Personal Memory, and the list is reordered by that
rank. Anonymous callers (no ``user_id``) get the editorial order.
Full angle detail — dossier body + member venues with Take headlines.
Angles this venue is a member of. Powers the breadcrumb chip.

schema.title: Response List Venue Angles Api Venues  Venue Id  Angles Get

get.description:

see which models a given subsystem is spending on.

Cost estimates use the same per-token rates as ``backend/core/logging_config.py``.

post.description:
Send a message to the concierge for this trip.

Delegates to the DB-backed conversation session. Creates the trip's
group conversation on first call.

Optional ``X-Idempotency-Key`` header deduplicates retries: a second
call with the same key returns the cached response (if the original
completed) or 409 Conflict (if still processing).

Send a message and stream the response via Server-Sent Events.

Streams text deltas as they arrive from the LLM, then sends a final
event with metadata (tool calls, token counts, etc.).

Event types:
    - ``text_delta``: partial text chunk (``{"text": "..."}``).
    - ``metadata``: final turn metadata, sent once after the reply completes.
    - ``error``: if an exception occurs during processing.

Optional ``X-Idempotency-Key`` header deduplicates retries. A repeat
request with the same key whose original is still streaming returns
HTTP 409 (before the SSE stream begins); a repeat whose original
completed returns the cached response as a single metadata event.

get.description: Get conversation history for this trip from the DB.

schema.title: Response Get History Api Trips  Trip Id  Messages History Get

get.description: Get basic session info (turn count, message count).

post.description:
Close the conversation session for this trip.

Triggers post-session reflection for all participants.

schema.title: Response Close Session Endpoint Api Trips  Trip Id  Messages Close Post

post.description:
Create a personal side conversation linked to this trip.

Allows a user to have a private 1:1 chat with the concierge about
trip-specific topics (budget concerns, social dynamics, personal
preferences) without the group seeing the conversation.

The agent will have full access to trip context (itinerary, preferences,
member profiles) but will skip group coordination features.

get.description: List collections for the Discover Guides row.

schema.title: Response List Collections Endpoint Api Collections Get

get.description: Fetch a single collection + its hydrated, ordered members.

post.description: Create a new conversation (group or personal).

get.description: Get a conversation with its participants.
Get paginated message history for a conversation.

schema.title: Response Get Messages Api Conversations  Conversation Id  Messages Get

post.description:
Send a message in a conversation.

If ``trigger_agent`` is True (default), the concierge processes the
message and returns its reply. If False, the message is persisted as a
user-to-user message and no agent reply is generated — useful for group
chat where users want to talk to each other.

Optional ``X-Idempotency-Key`` header deduplicates retries (only
applies when ``trigger_agent`` is True; user-to-user messages don't
need retry protection at this layer).

schema.title: Response Send Message Api Conversations  Conversation Id  Messages Post

post.description:
Send a message and stream the response via Server-Sent Events.

Streams text deltas as they arrive from the LLM, then sends a final
event with metadata (tool calls, token counts, etc.).

If ``trigger_agent`` is False, returns a single SSE event with the
persisted message ID (no streaming needed).

Event types:
    - ``text_delta``: partial text chunk (``{"text": "..."}``).
    - ``metadata``: final turn metadata, sent once after the reply completes.
    - ``user_message``: when trigger_agent=False, the persisted message.
    - ``error``: if an exception occurs during processing.

Stamp the conversation's ``last_location`` with a fresh reading.

Participants only — the conversation's GPS is private to its
members. Mobile clients piggyback location on message sends for
the common path; this endpoint exists for background pushes
(foreground event, significant-change updates) where there's no
message to attach to.

Idempotent-ish: latest write wins. Readings older than 15 minutes
are filtered at read time by the origin resolver, so stale pushes
don't pollute — but callers shouldn't push stale readings anyway.

Promote a pre-trip conversation into a committed trip.

Reads the conversation's ``intent_state`` scratchpad, creates a trip
row with best-effort field mapping (title / place_id / dates),
links them both ways, copies conversation participants as trip
members, and stamps ``intent_state.phase='committed'``.

Only the conversation creator may promote — the resulting trip is
owned by that user (``trip.created_by = conversation.created_by``)
so allowing anyone else to drive this would let an attacker spawn
trips under a victim's name. The rest of the conversations router
is unauthenticated (house style for a separate reason), but
promotion is uniquely destructive and earns its gate.

Fails with:
- 403 if the caller isn't the conversation creator
- 400 on not-found / home / already-promoted / not-ready

post.description:
Mint a new invite token for a trip.

Gated on ``trip_has_substance`` — if the trip is a blank slate the
snapshot would be generic, which the Intake Philosophy doc is
explicit about avoiding (§6: generic first message permanently
poisons the relationship).

get.description: List all invites for a trip. Organizer-only.

schema.title: Response List Invites Api Trips  Trip Id  Invites Get

delete.description: Revoke a trip invite. Organizer-only, idempotent.

post.description:
Mint an invite for a pre-trip conversation.

Gated on ``conversation_has_substance`` — mirrors the trip-invite
gate but reads from intent_state rather than trip briefs. Keeps us
from sending friends a landing page that says nothing about the
trip being shaped.

get.description: List all invites for a conversation. Creator-only.

schema.title: Response List Conversation Invites Api Conversations  Conversation Id  Invites Get

delete.description: Revoke a conversation invite. Creator-only, idempotent.

get.description:
Render the landing page for an invite token. Public endpoint.

Branches on scope: trip invites render the committed-trip summary
with a Haiku-generated snapshot (cached). Conversation invites
(Phase 4, pre-trip group chat) render a lightweight summary built
directly from intent_state — no LLM call, no caching needed, since
the substance bar is low and the content is already structured.

post.description:
Record pre-auth chip answers on the invite.

Public endpoint — the invitee hasn't signed up yet. The payload is
merged into the invite's pending_intake JSONB and handed to the
concierge at accept-time so the conversation picks up continuously.

Consume the invite and add the authenticated user as a member.

Branches on scope: trip invites add the user to trip_members and log
an ``invite.consumed`` trip_event. Conversation invites (Phase 4)
add the user to conversation_participants; on promotion those
participants are copied over to trip_members automatically (see
promotion.py). Event logging for conversation invites is deferred
— we don't have a conversation_events table yet and the audit gap
at this pre-trip stage is acceptable.
Handle a lookup query and return a fast answer.
Add a user to a trip.

schema.title: Response List Members Api Trips  Trip Id  Members Get

delete.description: Remove a user from a trip.

get.description: Get the user's global notification preferences (creates defaults if absent).

patch.description: Update one or more fields on the user's notification preferences.

get.description: Get the per-trip notification state (includes cadence_override).

patch.description:
Set (or clear) the per-trip cadence override for a specific user.

Only the user themselves or the trip organizer may call this.
Pass cadence_override=null to clear and inherit from the user's global preference.

get.description: List change proposals for a trip.

schema.title: Response List Proposals Api Trips  Trip Id  Proposals Get

get.description: Get full details of a change proposal.

post.description: Cast a vote on a proposal.

Resolve a proposal (accept, reject, supersede, or withdraw).

Only trip organizers can resolve. Members can contribute signal
through conversation; the organizer makes the call.

Kick off a quick research pass in the background.

The instant answer should have already been returned via POST /api/lookup/.
This endpoint triggers a short graph run (3 iterations, ~1 minute) that
produces a dossier and persists structured data.

Kick off a deep research pass in the background.

Runs the full research graph (up to 15 iterations by default) with quality
gate, dossier writing, and persistence. Use profile_overrides for
curation-level runs (e.g. {"quality_threshold": 0.95, "max_iterations": 50}).
Save an entity for the user.

get.description: List a user's saves, newest first. Optionally filter by entity_type.

schema.title: Response List Saves Api Users  User Id  Saves Get

post.description:
Hybrid search across venues and events.

Venues: Qdrant-native search (BM25-only for short queries, BM25+dense
hybrid with server-side RRF for longer/descriptive queries).

Events: Postgres keyword search with pg_trgm similarity scoring.

Results from both sources are merged using Reciprocal Rank Fusion.

get.description:
Fast typeahead autocomplete for the search bar.

Returns suggestions across 5 entity types (venues, events, sites,
neighborhoods, destinations) ranked by pg_trgm similarity.
Designed for <100ms latency.

Find venues and sites near a geographic point.

Uses PostGIS ST_DWithin with GiST index — typically <30ms.

post.description:
Generate (or fetch cached) a Take for an entity.

Phase 1: venues, researching mode, sync response.
Phase 2: adds reviewing_plan mode + trip context.

get.description: Fetch a Take by ID. Used for reloading a specific cached Take.
List all trips for a user (via membership).

schema.title: Response List User Trips Api Users  User Id  Trips Get

get.description: Get a single trip by ID.

post.description:
Create a new trip in ideation status.

The creator is automatically added as an organizer so they have
authority to finalize proposals, edit trip shape, etc.

Archive a trip and return its conversation to drafting.

The two-way door from promotion. Trip row stays (status='archived')
for audit / undo. The primary conversation is unlinked and its
intent_state.phase set back to 'drafting' so the agent resumes
exploration without losing history.

Only trip members can decommit. The trip doesn't have to be 'ideation' —
later statuses can also be decommitted (e.g. user changed their mind
after confirming).

Email the most recent daily digest for this trip to the caller.

On-demand only in this phase — we don't push digests on a
schedule because the cadence decision (daily? weekly? per-trip?)
is a product question we haven't resolved. This endpoint lets a
user pull their digest to their inbox whenever they want, so the
digest engine's output actually reaches them.

Gates:
- Actor must be a trip member (existing ``_require_trip_member``)
- Actor's ``email`` must be non-null (from Clerk)
- At least one daily digest must exist (the engine populates these
  via the ``_run_digest_loop`` background task)

get.description:
Get all daily digests for a trip, ordered by day number.

Returns the AI-generated day summaries that power the Memory screen.
Only includes ``daily`` digest type — not trip_summary or planning_context.

schema.title: Response Get Trip Digests Api Trips  Trip Id  Digests Get

post.description:
Record where this trip is based, for a given date range.

Trip-member-gated — anyone on the trip can declare the base,
matching existing trip-scoped auth patterns. The concierge will
call this (or a tool backed by it) when the user answers
"where are you staying?" on promotion.

get.description: List all stays for a trip. Default is active only.

schema.title: Response List Trip Accommodations Api Trips  Trip Id  Accommodations Get

schema.title: Response Get Trip Itinerary Api Trips  Trip Id  Itinerary Get

post.description:
Record a traveler debrief and queue a post-trip reflection into Personal Memory.

Persists a ``trip_debrief_submitted`` user event (trip-scoped) and runs the
same reflection pass as automatic trip completion, with the debrief text
injected into the prompt so it cannot be missed by cursor timing.

get.description:
Return the authenticated user's profile.

The ``get_current_user`` dependency handles JWT verification and
user resolution. In dev mode (SKIP_AUTH=true), returns the default
dev user.

patch.description:
Update the user's editable profile fields (display_name, email).

Returns the updated row. Raises 404 if the user disappears between
auth resolution and the write (essentially impossible) and 409 if
the new email collides with another user.

post.description:
Set or change the user's phone number.

Clears any prior verification state — changing the number requires
a fresh verification before SMS will be delivered. Pass
``phone_number=null`` to remove the number entirely.

Generate a fresh OTP and send it via SMS.

Subsequent calls within the 10-minute expiry invalidate the prior
code (fresh OTP). No rate limit beyond what Twilio enforces —
worth adding server-side throttling when real usage warrants.
Submit the OTP to mark the phone number as verified.

Register (or refresh) a push token for the authenticated user.

Upserts on the token — the same physical device re-registering just
bumps ``last_seen_at`` and re-enables the row. Use this at app
start and after the OS rotates the token.

get.description: List push devices for the authenticated user.

schema.title: Response List Devices Endpoint Api Users  User Id  Devices Get

delete.description: Hard-delete a device registration (e.g. on user logout).

get.description:
Return the user's ambient home conversation, creating on miss.

The home conversation is the trip-less chat where the concierge
relationship lives between trips — inspiration, cross-trip questions,
late-night musings. One per user; bootstrapped at user creation.
This endpoint also backfills for any user who predates the bootstrap.

get.description: Basic liveness check — always returns 200 if the process is running.

Readiness check — verifies Postgres and Qdrant are reachable.

Returns 200 with component status if all healthy, 503 if any are down.
Use this for K8s readiness probes / Cloud Run health checks.

Report status of all background asyncio tasks.

Returns per-task status: running | disabled | done | cancelled | error.
Useful for confirming that DISABLE_LLM_BACKGROUND_LOOPS is working or
that a task hasn't silently crashed.

AccommodationWriteRequest.title: AccommodationWriteRequest
AccommodationWriteRequest.description:
Create or replace a trip accommodation.

Exactly one of ``accommodation_id`` or ``(freeform_lat, freeform_lng)``
must be provided. The server-side Pydantic validator and DB CHECK
both enforce this — the client gets a 422 or 400 on inconsistency.

AlternativeOption.title: AlternativeOption
AlternativeOption.description:
One alternative option a proposal may offer alongside the primary pick.

``extra="allow"`` keeps legacy fields (e.g. site_id, experience_id)
readable for older proposals.

AngleDetailAngle.title: AngleDetailAngle
AngleDetailAngle.description: Full angle payload — preview fields + long-form dossier body.

AngleMember.title: AngleMember
AngleMember.description:
A single exemplar venue of an angle, joined with venue display fields
for API responses. The shape the browse surface actually renders.

AnglePreview.title: AnglePreview
AnglePreview.description:
List-shape — what appears on a Discover row or a venue breadcrumb.

``title`` resolves server-side to ``display_title or title`` so clients
never need to know the distinction between the research hypothesis
title and the editorial display title.

source_url.title: Source Url
source_url.description: URL of the image to rehost.

curator_ref.title: Curator Ref
curator_ref.description: Curator identifier, e.g. 'curator:alex'. Free-form for now.

Caveat.title: Caveat
Caveat.description:
Structured because caveats get special UI treatment on the phone —
they can't be buried in the body (allergen warnings, accessibility, etc.).

CollectionDetail.title: CollectionDetail
CollectionDetail.description: Full collection payload: metadata + ordered hydrated members.

CollectionSummary.title: CollectionSummary
CollectionSummary.description: Guides-row card payload: just what the row needs to render.

CreateSideChatRequest.title: CreateSideChatRequest
CreateSideChatRequest.description: Create a personal side conversation linked to a trip.

CreateSideChatResponse.title: CreateSideChatResponse
CreateSideChatResponse.description: Response after creating a side chat.

DeepResearchRequest.title: DeepResearchRequest
DeepResearchRequest.description: Request to run a deep research pass (background, minutes).

DigestRunResponse.title: DigestRunResponse
DigestRunResponse.description: Result of a manual digest run.

EntityTake.title: EntityTake
EntityTake.description: A cached Take row — what the DB stores and the API returns.

ExpenseShareInput.title: ExpenseShareInput
ExpenseShareInput.description: Used in ExpenseCreate/Update to define shares inline.

HeadingBlock.title: HeadingBlock
HeadingBlock.description:
A section heading. ``level`` is 2 or 3 — we deliberately don't
expose h1 because the Take headline is the de facto h1 of the card.

HydratedMember.title: HydratedMember
HydratedMember.description:
One member with its entity type tag + lightweight entity payload.

The entity payload is shaped by the DB layer; the shape varies per
entity type (a venue has `name`, a dossier has `angle`, etc.). The
mobile app switches on `entity_type` to render.

Hypothesis.title: Hypothesis
Hypothesis.description:
Internal-only. The LLM's working claim about the user and its evidence.

NOT surfaced to the user. Used for:
- Debugging + eval ("why did the Take emphasize X?")
- Offline analysis (aggregating which hypothesis categories correlate
  with saves vs dismisses, feeding Personal Memory refresh)
- Grounding validation (post-generation check that every hypothesis
  references real evidence)

`evidence_refs` are opaque ID strings scoped by `evidence_type`:
- personal_memory: the Personal Memory version id or a section marker
- past_trip: trip_id
- angle_affinity: angle_id
- group_signal: group_profile_version id
- saved_entity: entity_id previously saved

IntakePayload.title: IntakePayload
IntakePayload.description:
Bulk chip payload for minimal onboarding.

Each field is optional. Sections use replace-within-section semantics:
submitting ``dietary=["vegan"]`` deactivates any other active dietary rows
for the user. Omitting a field leaves that section untouched, so callers
can update one section without disturbing others. An empty list clears
the section.

InviteConversationSummary.title: InviteConversationSummary
InviteConversationSummary.description:
Landing-page facts for a conversation-scoped invite (pre-trip).

Lighter than :class:`InviteTripSummary` — the conversation is still
exploratory, so we render what the intent scratchpad has captured
so far rather than committed trip details.

InviteIntakeRequest.title: InviteIntakeRequest
InviteIntakeRequest.description: Chip answers and optional contact info captured pre-auth.

InviteSnapshot.title: InviteSnapshot
InviteSnapshot.description: Haiku-generated landing-page content for one invite view.

InviteTripSummary.title: InviteTripSummary
InviteTripSummary.description: Non-generated facts shown on the landing page alongside the snapshot.

ListBlock.title: ListBlock
ListBlock.description: An ordered or bulleted list. Each item supports inline markdown.

LocationReading.title: LocationReading
LocationReading.description:
A single GPS reading from a client device.

Written to ``conversations.metadata_.last_location`` and read by
the concierge's origin resolver (15-min TTL). Piggybacks on message
send requests so the concierge always sees the freshest available
location at turn time without a separate round-trip.

MemoryResetResponse.title: MemoryResetResponse
MemoryResetResponse.description: Counts of rows deleted per source table — useful for confirmation UI.

NearbyItem.title: NearbyItem
NearbyItem.description: A venue or site near a given point.

NearbyResponse.title: NearbyResponse
NearbyResponse.description: Nearby venues/sites around a geographic point.

ParagraphBlock.title: ParagraphBlock
ParagraphBlock.description: A prose paragraph. Supports inline markdown in ``text``.

PendingVote.title: PendingVote
PendingVote.description: A proposal the user hasn't voted on.

force.title: Force
force.description: Override the readiness gate. By default we require at least one destination and a date window in the intent scratchpad; set force=True to promote anyway (e.g. for testing).

PromoteToTripRequest.title: PromoteToTripRequest
PromoteToTripRequest.description: Explicit promotion of a pre-trip conversation into a committed trip.

ProposalDetail.title: ProposalDetail
ProposalDetail.description:
Full proposal detail.

When ``proposed_entity_type`` + ``proposed_entity_id`` are set, the
mobile app deep-links the proposal card into that entity's detail
view, where a per-viewer personalized Take is fetched via
``POST /api/takes``. Both are null for proposals that don't point
at a single entity (e.g. pure reschedule, "add a day").

``voting_enabled`` mirrors the trip-level flag — the mobile app uses
it to decide whether to render the vote widget (enabled) or a
reaction-only view with an "ask organizer to resolve" affordance
(disabled).

ProposalImpactAnalysis.title: ProposalImpactAnalysis
ProposalImpactAnalysis.description:
Structured trade-off payload for a change proposal.

All fields are optional so the concierge can fill in as much or as
little as warranted by the proposal stakes. ``extra="allow"`` preserves
legacy keys (e.g. ``new_time``, ``new_title``, ``new_notes``) written
by older proposals and consumed by ``proposal_apply._apply_*``.

ProposalSummary.title: ProposalSummary
ProposalSummary.description:
Lightweight proposal for list views.

Deliberately omits ``proposed_entity_*`` — the deep-link affordance
is a detail-page concern, not a list-row concern.

QuickResearchRequest.title: QuickResearchRequest
QuickResearchRequest.description: Request to run a quick research pass (~1 minute).

ReactRequest.title: ReactRequest
ReactRequest.description: React to a structured message (reaction_card or vote_widget).

move.title: Move
move.description: One-line verdict — 'the move is X because Y'. Opinionated.

why_for_group.title: Why For Group
why_for_group.description: Why this fits THIS group (energy, constraints, dynamic).

why_for_person.title: Why For Person
why_for_person.description: Why this fits the requester specifically (personal memory, prior preferences).

what_to_notice.title: What To Notice
what_to_notice.description: Connoisseur notes — what to look for, order, ask about.

what_to_skip.title: What To Skip
what_to_skip.description: What to avoid or deprioritize at this place.

timing_note.title: Timing Note
timing_note.description: When to go, how long, reservation tip, time-of-day cue.

RecommendationPayload.title: RecommendationPayload
RecommendationPayload.description: Structured reasoning attached to a concierge recommendation.

RegisterDeviceRequest.title: RegisterDeviceRequest
RegisterDeviceRequest.description: Mobile client calls this on login / app start to register its push token.

ResearchTaskResponse.title: ResearchTaskResponse
ResearchTaskResponse.description: Acknowledgement that a research task has been enqueued.

ResolveRequest.title: ResolveRequest
ResolveRequest.description:
Resolve (accept/reject) a change proposal.

The resolver is the authenticated actor — they must be an
organizer of the trip. No ``resolved_by`` in the body; trusting
the client to name the resolver would bypass authz.

experience_type.title: Experience Type
experience_type.description: concert, theater, comedy, festival, tour, etc.

entity_types.title: Entity Types
entity_types.description: Entity types to search: ["venue", "experience", "site", "accommodation"]. "event" is accepted as a legacy alias for "experience" during migration. Defaults to all.

SearchRequest.title: SearchRequest
SearchRequest.description: Unified search request — drives the Discover tab search bar.

SearchResponse.title: SearchResponse
SearchResponse.description: Unified search response with fused results.

SearchResultItem.title: SearchResultItem
SearchResultItem.description: A single search result — venue, experience, site, or accommodation.

SessionInfoResponse.title: SessionInfoResponse
SessionInfoResponse.description: Basic info about the current session.

SetPhoneRequest.title: SetPhoneRequest
SetPhoneRequest.description: E.164 format (e.g. +14155551234). Pass null to clear.

SuggestResponse.title: SuggestResponse
SuggestResponse.description: Autocomplete suggestions for the search bar.

TakeGenerationRequest.title: TakeGenerationRequest
TakeGenerationRequest.description: Body for POST /api/takes — request to generate (or fetch cached) a Take.

body.title: Body
body.description: Ordered list of content blocks. Paragraphs are the default; use headings only when the Take has 3+ distinct sections; use lists when enumerating 3+ items that read poorly as prose.

TakePayload.title: TakePayload
TakePayload.description:
The full Take content — what gets rendered (and what gets logged).

Tier 1 (Curator) and Tier 2 (Personal) share this shape. Personal Takes
include grounded `hypotheses` with real evidence refs; Curator Takes
leave `hypotheses` empty.

TakeResponse.title: TakeResponse
TakeResponse.description: Response shape for non-streaming Take requests.

TripContext.title: TripContext
TripContext.description: Trip-scoped context for filtering and relevance.

TripDebriefSubmit.title: TripDebriefSubmit
TripDebriefSubmit.description: Structured post-trip debrief — funnels explicit traveler signal into memory.

TripDigest.title: TripDigest
TripDigest.description:
A daily, trip-summary, or planning-context structured digest.

`content` is the LLM's structured JSON output; `rendered_markdown` is a
pre-formatted textual view populated at generation time so consumers
(planner prompt, admin UI) don't need to re-render the payload.

UserMessageResponse.title: UserMessageResponse
UserMessageResponse.description: Response when a message is persisted without triggering an agent reply.

UserUpdate.title: UserUpdate
UserUpdate.description: Partial update — only include fields you want to change.

Verdict.title: Verdict
Verdict.description:
Opinion layer. Derived from signals, not free LLM judgment.

`derived_from` lists the signal categories the verdict is grounded in,
for traceability and debugging.

VoiceTokenRequest.title: VoiceTokenRequest
VoiceTokenRequest.description: Request a LiveKit access token for voice chat.

VoiceTokenResponse.title: VoiceTokenResponse
VoiceTokenResponse.description: LiveKit connection credentials for the client.

location.description: Optional current GPS reading from the client. Written to conversation metadata before the turn runs; read by the concierge's distance tool when from_origin='current_location'.

backend__api__routes__chat__SendMessageR.title: SendMessageRequest
backend__api__routes__chat__SendMessageR.description: A single message from a user to the concierge.

backend__api__routes__chat__SendMessageR.title: SendMessageResponse
backend__api__routes__chat__SendMessageR.description: What the concierge returns after processing a message.

trigger_agent.title: Trigger Agent
trigger_agent.description: If False, persist the message without triggering an agent reply. Useful for user-to-user chat in group conversations.

location.description: Optional current GPS reading from the client. Stored on the conversation's metadata before the turn runs.

backend__api__routes__conversations__Sen.title: SendMessageRequest
backend__api__routes__conversations__Sen.description: Send a message in a conversation.

backend__api__routes__conversations__Sen.title: SendMessageResponse
backend__api__routes__conversations__Sen.description: Agent reply after processing a message.
